definitions.text: A solid object in the shape of a circle. A circumscribing object, (roughly) circular and hollow, looking like an annual ring, earring, finger ring etc.
examples: His brother gifted him a ring for the engagement.

definitions.text: A circumscribing object, (roughly) circular and hollow, looking like an annual ring, earring, finger ring etc.

examples: The inner city was ringed with dingy industrial areas.

definitions.text: To make an incision around; to girdle; to cut away a circular tract of bark from a tree in order to kill it.
examples: They ringed the trees to make the clearing easier next year.

definitions.text: To attach a ring to, especially for identification.
examples: We managed to ring 22 birds this morning.

definitions.text: The resonant sound of a bell, or a sound resembling it.
examples: The church bell's ring could be heard the length of the valley.
The ring of hammer on anvil filled the air.
The name has a nice ring to it.

definitions.text: A sound or appearance that is characteristic of something.
examples: Her statements in court had a ring of falsehood.

definitions.text: Of a bell, etc., to produce a resonant sound.
examples: The bells were ringing in the town.

definitions.text: To make (a bell, etc.) produce a resonant sound.
examples: The deliveryman rang the doorbell to drop off a parcel.
They rang a Christmas carol on their handbells.

definitions.text: An algebraic structure which consists of a set with two binary operations: an additive operation and a multiplicative operation, such that the set is an abelian group under the additive operation, a monoid under the multiplicative operation, and such that the multiplicative operation is distributive with respect to the additive operation.
examples: The set of integers, Z {\displaystyle \mathbb {Z} } , is the prototypical ring.

definitions.text: An algebraic structure as above, but only required to be a semigroup under the multiplicative operation, that is, there need not be a multiplicative identity element.
examples: The definition of ring without unity allows, for instance, the set 2 Z {\displaystyle 2\mathbb {Z} } of even integers to be a ring.

definitions.text: A family of sets that is closed under finite unions and set-theoretic differences.
A family of sets closed under finite union and finite intersection.
